key_insight: The paper formalizes core q-series machinery in Lean and uses it to machine-check landmark identities such as the Jacobi Triple Product and Rogers-Ramanujan identities.
engineering_relevance: It provides reusable verified foundations for theorem proving workflows in computational mathematics, enabling future formalization of deeper results in modular forms, mock theta functions, and related areas.
method: Built Lean-friendly definitions for q-Pochhammer symbols and q-binomial coefficients.
Developed formalized versions of Bailey's Lemma and other q-series primitives.
Proved key benchmark identities end-to-end in the proof assistant.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports fully verified proofs of the Jacobi Triple Product formula and the Rogers-Ramanujan identities as validation of the formalization framework.
watch_item: A main implementation concern is reconciling formal algebraic manipulations with analytic convergence assumptions in a scalable way.
paper_title: Formalized $q$-series: The Rogers-Ramanujan Identities and Beyond
